Glean Adoption-OS

Amplify Education

A proactive AIOM workspace for messy real-world account management: unified signals, next-best-action guidance, recommended sessions/docs/tools, release-intelligence watchpoints, and engagement + comp-risk alerts.

What to capture in Prism:
Adoption OS phase backbone

Phase objective
Current milestone

Open adoption activities

Phase-relevant assets

Function phase status

Weekly priority is a summary layer. The operating model is the Adoption OS phase, and different client functions may be moving through different phases at the same time.

Needs attention now

What changed and what matters

Recommended doc

Open resource
Recommended Glean resource

Recommended asset

Upcoming release signals

Communicate before customers get surprised

Engagement + comp risk

Commercial guardrails

These are the AIOM-friendly guardrails. Under the hood, Assistant + Agents health and Prism-derived knowledge now feed the same recommendation system rather than sitting in a separate technical dashboard.

Signal intake coverage

Source registry

AI health + knowledge

Assistant, Agents, and Prism

What Prism already tells us about client health:
Health signal

Risk pattern

Prior action outcome

Repeated blocker

Momentum since last touch

Play from history

This keeps the AIOM operating surface focused while Prism-derived health and account memory inform the recommendation.

Future intelligence layer

AI Fluency layer

Q2/Q3 placeholder

AI Fluency is planned as a Q2/Q3 enrichment layer and is not required for V1. Today, the artifact uses Adoption OS phase, client signals, existing tools, and Prism context to recommend the strategic action and best next step.

What it will add

Capability context

Function-level fluency signals can explain why a team is ready, stalled, or likely to regress.

How it will fit

Strategy refinement

Fluency data should tune the recommended motion without replacing the Adoption OS backbone.

V1 boundary

Do not block launch

Missing AI Fluency data should be treated as future enrichment, not a reason to delay the Glean artifact.

Ready-to-go resources

Document library by adoption stage

Templates stay phase-aware, but the recommendation engine picks the one doc you should open first.

Agents, skills, and launchers

AIOM tool library

Launch behavior is explicit, but the tools stay inside the familiar AIOM workflow rather than becoming a separate technical experience.

Signal inbox

All signals for this account

Signal-to-action framework

How the workspace decides

Who needs me this week

Portfolio ranking

Confirm the suggested client roster before portfolio intelligence loads.

Rank Account Attention Phase Engagement Comp risk Hours left Aggregation Next move
Operating model

What this version now does

1

Keeps the AIOM-friendly dashboard

The primary workflow stays human-readable and action-oriented: what changed, what matters, what to do next, and what asset or tool to use.

2

Updates the data pipeline underneath

Assistant health, Agent health, Prism-derived knowledge, and widget maturity are now part of the underlying model instead of requiring a separate dashboard experience.

3

Keeps model + QA secondary

The raw model and widget audit are still available for PM, Eng, and QA, but they no longer sit in the main AIOM workflow.

4

Expands the portfolio again

The toolbar and portfolio now use the fuller client set so the dashboard feels like a true operating surface rather than a narrowed demo.

Prism workspace

Capture the most relevant information for this account

Canvas-safe inline view

This is a dedicated Prism memory and health loop for the selected account. It shows what Prism already tells us about client health, then captures whether the recommended action worked so the next recommendation can improve.

Most relevant context
AIOM capture
Notes are held in memory while this artifact is open.
Top signals informing this capture
Copy into Prism

Save this summary into Prism after the customer touch so the account memory includes the recommended action, actual outcome, remaining blocker, commitments, and follow-up loop.

Canvas interactive content does not provide persistent browser storage, so capture notes are session-only in this artifact. Durable notes should be copied into the account workspace or captured through a Glean Agent flow.

View data model and widget status

Starter schema

Data model

This is the underlying contract that powers the Adoption OS intelligence surface.

Static widget audit

What is live vs. what is still demo-driven

This stays secondary so AIOMs can trust the interface without being forced into a model-first workflow.

Embedded Prism experience

Prism snapshot

Knowledge coverage
Refresh + signals

Derived account knowledge
